data.title: Nippon Steel: Conversion From Blast Furnace Would Require Capital Investment, Lead to Higher Production Costs
India's Birlasoft drops on BOBCaps' downgrade to "sell"
data.description:
** Birlasoft shares drop 4.7% to 400.90 rupees

** BOBCaps downgrades tier II IT services provider to "sell" from "hold", cuts PT from 402 rupees to 342 rupees

** Brokerage's PT lowest among 11 analysts; median PT at 448 rupees - data compiled by LSEG

** BOBCaps calls BIRS "weakest Q4 performer" among tier II IT firms that include Persistent Systems and Mphasis

** Says BIRS' weak FY26 start and subdued deal wins would mean flattish revenue; flags co's high risk to discretionary spends

** Q4 revenue was below its estimates, hurt by extended furloughs and project ramp downs - BOBCaps

** BIRS, MBFL "buy"; PERS "hold" - avg ratings per data compiled by LSEG

** YTD decline - BIRS 28%, PERS 13%, MBFL 10%

data.title: Sanofi, Regeneron's Itepekimab Delivers Mixed Results in Late-Stage Trials
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Sanofi and Regeneron said that drug candidate Itepekimab met the primary goal in one of two chronic obstructive pulmonary disease phase 3 studies, but didn't hit the main objective of a second trial.

The French pharmaceutical company on Friday said that Itepekimab in former smokers with inadequately controlled chronic obstructive pulmonary disease met the primary endpoint of a statistically significant reduction in moderate or severe acute exacerbations compared to placebo of 27% at week 52.

However, a second phase three study didn't meet primary endpoint, although a benefit was seen earlier in the trial, it said.

Itepekimab was generally well tolerated in both studies. Sanofi and Regeneron are assessing the data and will discuss with regulatory authorities to evaluate next steps, it said.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: UOB-Kay Hian Purchases Additional Shares in Thai Subsidiary
data.description:
UOB-Kay Hian , through its unit UOB-Kay Hian (BVI), acquired 8,500 additional shares in its Thai subsidiary, UOB Kay Hian Securities Thailand (SET:UOBKH), at 5.4676 baht apiece.

Following the purchase, the total number of shares held directly by UOB-Kay Hian in the Thai entity remains unchanged at 70.65%, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

data.title: Synopsys halts China sales due to US export restrictions, internal memo shows
data.description:
Semiconductor design software firm Synopsys has told staff in China to halt services and sales in the country and stop taking new orders to comply with new U.S. export restrictions, according to an internal letter reviewed by Reuters.

The U.S. has ordered a broad swathe of companies to stop shipping goods to China without a license and revoked licenses already granted to certain suppliers, Reuters reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Products affected include design software and chemicals for semiconductors, they said.

Synopsys on Thursday suspended its annual and quarterly forecasts after it received a letter from the Bureau of Industry and Security of the U.S. Department of Commerce, informing it of new export restrictions related to China.

The internal letter sent to staff in China on Friday said "based on our initial interpretation, these new restrictions broadly prohibit the sales of our products and services in China and are effective as of May 29, 2025."

To ensure compliance, Synopsys said it was blocking sales and fulfillment in China and halting new orders until it receives further clarification.

The measures affect all customers in China, including employees of global customers working at sites in China and Chinese military users wherever they are located, the letter added.

The steps Synopsys is taking in light of the new restrictions have not been previously reported.

Synopsys did not immediately reply to a request for comment.

Alongside Cadence and Siemens EDA, Synopsys is among the top three companies that dominate electronic design automation (EDA) software that chipmakers can use to design semiconductors used in everything from smartphones to computers and cars.

Restricting Chinese firms' access to EDA tools would be a big blow to the industry as Chinese chip design customers heavily rely on top-of-the-line U.S. software.

Synopsys, Cadence and Siemens's Mentor Graphics control more than 70% of China's EDA market, Chinese state news agency Xinhua reported in April.

Chinese companies that have said they use Synopsys and Cadence software include design firm Brite Semiconductor, Zhuhai Jieli and semiconductor IP portfolio provider VeriSilicon.

The letter sent to staff in China on Friday also said that Chinese customers' access to its customer support portal SolvNetPlus had been disabled.

data.title: Beamtree Holdings Enters Strategic Partnership with NHS Confederation in the UK, Shares Rise 4%
data.description:
Beamtree Holdings entered a strategic partnership with the NHS Confederation, which represents approximately 120 NHS Trusts in England, for a five-year term with an extension option for another five years, according to a Friday ASX filing.

The partnership is expected to be launched near the end of the year, with a membership price of 50,000 pounds sterling per annum for each hospital trust. The first 500,000 pounds of membership income will be 100% remitted to Beamtree to cover the cost of establishing the platform in the UK, and thereafter will be shared equally.

Beamtree's expected investment is expected to be around AU$1.5 million over the current fiscal year and fiscal year 2026.

Its shares rose 4% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Transurban Says Underground Road Project's Financing Arm Secures AU$200 Million Facility
data.description:
Transurban Group said that WestConnex Finance, the financing arm of its WestConnex underground road infrastructure project, has secured AU$200 million bank facility, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The facility is set to mature in May 2035, the filing said.

data.title: Holcim Begins Work on EUR400 Million Cement Production Plant in Greece
data.description:
Holcim said Thursday it broke ground on its development of a 400 million-euro cement production plant in Milaki, Greece.

The Swiss building materials group is collaborating with French industrial gases company Air Liquide on the Olympus project, which was selected for a 125 million-euro grant from the European Union's Innovation Fund.

The facility is expected to have an annual production capacity of 2 million tons of near-zero cement from 2029.

data.title: Toyo Tire Shares Extend Gains, Now Up 5.6%
Dollar poised for fifth monthly drop on trade, fiscal uncertainty
data.description:
Key points:
  • Upbeat Tokyo core inflation underpins yen
  • Fed's preferred PCE inflation data awaited
  • Dollar index set for fifth-straight monthly decline

The U.S. dollar wobbled on Friday, heading for its fifth-straight monthly decline as traders braced for further uncertainty around trade policy and U.S. fiscal health while awaiting pivotal inflation reports later in the day.

The greenback had a choppy week, ending lower in the previous session after a federal court temporarily reinstated the most sweeping of President Donald Trump's tariffs, just a day after another court had ordered an immediate block on them.

Trump on Thursday said he hoped the Supreme Court would overturn the trade court's decision, while officials also indicated that they could employ other presidential powers to ensure the tariffs take effect.

The uncertainty around tariffs has taken a vice-like grip on markets as investors flee U.S. assets looking for alternatives, worried that Trump's erratic policies could challenge the strength and outperformance of U.S. markets.

"The (court) decision marks the beginning of a new source of uncertainty rather than the total closure of another," said Kyle Rodda, senior financial market analyst at Capital.com, noting that the mood in the market was cautious.

On Friday, the euro was slightly firmer at $1.1378, ahead of German inflation data for May while the Swiss franc was little changed at 0.8225 per dollar.

The U.S. currency though was set for monthly declines against the Swiss franc, the euro and the pound .

Thursday's weekly jobless claims and economic growth data did little to placate worries of a U.S. economic downturn. Investor focus will be on the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation data - the personal consumption expenditure (PCE) report - later on Friday.

Worries about fiscal debt levels in developed economies, highlighted by weak appetite for freshly issued longer-dated credit in the U.S. and in Japan, have also weighed.

The dollar index , which tracks the U.S. unit against a basket of six other currencies, was 0.16% higher at 99.416. The index was set for a decline of 0.25% in May, its fifth straight month in the red.

On the flip side, markets have taken note of emerging market assets. An index tracking emerging market currencies (.MIEM00000CUS) has gained 2.2% for the month - its biggest one-month rise since November 2023.

INFLATION WATCH

The Japanese yen firmed 0.3% to 143.80 per dollar after data showed underlying inflation in Tokyo hit a more than two-year high in May, keeping alive the chances of further interest rate hikes from the Bank of Japan.

"The BOJ is in a difficult position," said Min Joo Kang, senior economist at ING. "Inflationary pressures remain hot, while the economic recovery remains fragile — and facing strong headwinds from US tariffs."

However, the yen is on track for its first monthly decline against the dollar this year.

Markets are also on the lookout for clues on highly anticipated trade deals as the Trump-mandated July 9 deadline on tariffs draws near.

The U.S. PCE data is likely to show that inflation rose 2.2% in April, according to economists polled by Reuters, compared with a 2.3% increase in March.

The Fed tracks the PCE price measures for its 2% inflation target. Economists are forecasting a surge in inflation this year as the Trump administration's import duties raise the cost of goods.

Yields on longer-dated U.S. and Japanese bonds have eased this week, but still remain close to multi-month highs. Benchmark 10-year Treasury yields were last steady at 4.426%.

Elsewhere, the Australian dollar eased slightly to $0.6431, while the New Zealand dollar was last bought at$0.5968.

data.title: INST Magnetic New Material to Release 1.5 Million Shares from Restriction
data.description:
Baotou INST Magnetic New Material on June 4 will release 1,494,886 shares, accounting for 1.29% of the company's total post-initial public offering share capital, from restriction.

The shares were subject to a six-month lock-up as part of IPO rules, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Once released, the magnetic device manufacturer's unrestricted shares will increase to 25% of its total 115,931,880 shares.

data.title: SGX Adds CLSA Singapore to Clearing Members of Derivatives Market
data.description:
The Singapore Exchange confirmed CLSA Singapore as a clearing member to its derivatives market, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

CLSA has been a derivatives trading member since 2023 and is a wholly owned subsidiary of CITIC CLSA, the overseas platform of CITIC Securities.

data.title: Sanofi Posts Mixed Late-stage Data for COPD Treatment Itepekimab
data.description:
Sanofi on Friday reported mixed late-stage results for its investigational drug itepekimab in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, or COPD.

The phase 3 trials Aerify-1 and Aerify-2 evaluated itepekimab in former smokers with inadequately controlled COPD within a 52-week treatment period. The Aerify-1 study showed a statistically significant 27% reduction in the annualized rate of acute moderate or severe exacerbations compared with placebo at week 52, meeting the primary endpoint.

On the other hand, the Aerify-2 trial did not meet the same primary endpoint, although it showed a benefit earlier in the study.

France-listed pharmaceutical company Sanofi is jointly developing itepekimab with Regeneron. The companies are reviewing the study results and are in discussions with regulators to evaluate the next steps.

data.title: Planetel to Acquire Areatech di Coletta Josef Division
data.description:
Planetel (PLN.MI) said Thursday it agreed to buy a division of Areatech di Coletta Josef for a valuation of 300,000 euros.

The one-person business provides IT and TLC services and CCTV systems, as well as maintenance and installation of telephone and electronic systems.

Italy-listed telecommunications company Planetel expects the acquisition to close by June 30.

data.title: Work on $1 bln Trump Tower in Vietnam could start next year, source says
data.description:
Key points:
  • Eric Trump visited site for Ho Chi Minh City tower last week
  • Trump Organization will also operate $1.5 billion golf resort
  • Local partner would invest and develop projects, source says

Investment in a planned Trump Tower in Vietnam is estimated at around $1 billion and construction of the at least 60-storey skyscraper could start next year, a person familiar with the discussions told Reuters.

The building, for which plans are still preliminary, would be the second major project the Trump Organization, U.S. President Donald Trump's family business, is reviewing in Vietnam. The Southeast Asian country is currently in trade talks with Washington to avoid crippling 46% tariffs.

Eric Trump, senior vice president of the Trump Organization, visited Vietnam last week to survey the site for the tower in the country's southern business hub of Ho Chi Minh City and to attend the groundbreaking for a golf resort in northern Vietnam. The approval for the golf club, according to Prime Minister Pham Minh Chinh, had been expedited by the authorities.

The Trump Organization would operate the golf club, while its Vietnamese partner, real estate firm Kinhbac City , would focus on developing it with an estimated investment of $1.5 billion, the source said. The division of work had not been previously reported.

A similar arrangement is likely to be agreed for the Trump Tower, with the investment expected to come mostly from local partners, the source said, declining to be named because the information is not public.

The Trump Organization did not reply to a request for comment.

Kinhbac City, a leading industrial real estate developer in Vietnam, did not respond to a request for comment.

When it announced its collaboration with the Trump Organization in October, the company said the venture "will focus on developing 5-star hotels, championship-style golf courses, and luxurious residential estates and unparalleled amenities in Vietnam."

During his visit last week, Eric Trump toured the site where the skyscraper would be built and met local officials, the source said. That was in line with the schedule of his engagements with the city's authorities, according to a Vietnamese document seen by Reuters.

The tower would be built in Thu Thiem, an area under development in central Ho Chi Minh City where a new financial centre is planned to be built, the document from the city's authorities showed.

'ENVY OF ALL OF ASIA'

At the groundbreaking for the golf resort, Eric Trump said the projects to be developed in Vietnam would be "the envy of all of Asia and of the entire world", adding he would visit the country frequently to further the company's plans.

Approvals for the tower project are still being sought and no final decision had been made about when construction will start, the source said, adding that the aim was to have the groundbreaking next year.

The investment could be larger than $1 billion if permits are obtained for a building with more than 60 floors, the person said.

The Trump Organization operates several towers in the United States and across the world, but does not own all of them.

The flagship Trump Tower in the Manhattan neighbourhood of New York City was built in the 1980s. Others have since been built in Chicago, Florida and abroad, including in Turkey and the Philippines.

data.title: U.S. Treasurys Act Well as Economic Barometer — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. Treasurys have been a busy economic barometer as of late and the net effect of all the tumult is that the average yield on the U.S. Treasury index is about 20 basis points higher than before President Trump's tariff announcements on April 2, says Federated Hermes' R.J. Gallo in a note. U.S. budget deliberations are not yet finished and the Senate could end up cutting more spending from the bill, the senior portfolio manager for fixed income says. "The bond market would welcome any additional fiscal restraint added to the bill," he says. "Conversely, should Senate moderate push in the opposite direction to water down Medicaid cuts, the opposite net effect could occur, and Treasury yields may rise somewhat." (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Nippon Steel: Conversion to Electric Arc Furnace Would Significantly Cut CO2 Emissions
Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0543 GMT - U.S. Treasurys have been a busy economic barometer as of late and the net effect of all the tumult is that the average yield on the U.S. Treasury index is about 20 basis points higher than before President Trump's tariff announcements on April 2, says Federated Hermes' R.J. Gallo in a note. U.S. budget deliberations are not yet finished and the Senate could end up cutting more spending from the bill, the senior portfolio manager for fixed income says. "The bond market would welcome any additional fiscal restraint added to the bill," he says. "Conversely, should Senate moderate push in the opposite direction to water down Medicaid cuts, the opposite net effect could occur, and Treasury yields may rise somewhat." (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0508 GMT - A trade court ruling on Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs could limit the U.S. push to strike trade deals, Citi analysts say. While under appeal, markets may redraw some of their original assumptions, they write. Treasury Secretary Bessent told Fox News that the ruling hasn't had an impact on negotiations. "We have not seen any of that in terms of our trading partners, they are coming to us in good faith and trying to complete the deals before the 90-day pause ends," he said. "We've seen no change in their attitude in the past 48 hours." He characterized U.S.-China talks as "a bit stalled," adding that a Trump-Xi call is likely needed given their magnitude and complexity. Bessent said the two leaders have a "very good relationship" and expressed confidence that China will come to the table. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

data.title: Easing Government Bond Supply in Eurozone Likely to Support Duration in 2H — Market Talk
data.description: Bond issuance pressure in the eurozone is set to moderate meaningfully in 2H relative to 1H, and at the margin this should support European duration, Morgan Stanley Research's rates strategists say in a note. They forecast gross issuance in 2H to decrease by about 35% versus 1H, with issuance net of redemptions and of the European Central Bank's quantitative tightening halving versus 1H. The strategists forecast gross government bond issuance of 495 billion euros in 2H, down from 772 billion euros expected in 1H. "This should help provide some relief to European yields in 2H25," they say. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: New Zealand Shares Rise Amid Trump Tariff Reinstatement, New Zealand King Salmon Cuts Harvest Forecast
data.description:
New Zealand shares rose on Friday's close while most Asian indexes were down after a US federal appeals court temporarily reinstated President Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs.

The S&P/NZX 50 Index rose 1.12% or 137.58 points to close at 12,418.89.

A federal appeals court temporarily reinstated President Donald Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs on Thursday after a U.S. trade court ruled that he exceeded his authority in imposing the tariffs, Reuters reported.

In domestic news, housing and personal consumer lending in New Zealand grew to NZ$389.77 billion in April from NZ$388.21 billion in March, according to data from the Reserve Bank of New Zealand.

Further, the number of first home buyers accounted for nearly 25% of all property purchases between January and April, exceeding the long-term average of 21% to 22%, according to the Cotality-Westpac NZ First Home Buyer Report.

Also, New Zealand's consumer confidence weakened in May, highlighting risks to household spending amid global uncertainty from tariffs, according to a survey by ANZ Research and Roy Morgan Research.

Moreover, New Zealand's seasonally adjusted approved new dwellings declined 17% to 2,418 homes in April, following a 10% rise in March, Stats NZ data showed.

Meanwhile, insurance is now the fourth biggest financial burden for NZ households, rising from sixth place in late 2024, as premiums increase across house, contents, car, and health insurance, according to a Thursday consumer.org.nz statement.

In corporate news, New Zealand King Salmon Investments (, NZE: NZK) lowered its fiscal year 2026 harvest forecast in the range of 5,200 tonnes to 5,400 tonnes, from the previous guidance of 5,900 tonnes to 6,300 tonnes, following its decision to reduce harvest volumes for around four months.

Chorus said its fibre-regulated asset base remained steady at NZ$5.9 billion in the 2024 disclosure year.

data.title: Lukang Pharmaceutical's Azithromycin Tablets Passes China Regulatory Evaluation; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
Shandong Lukang Pharmaceutical's azithromycin tablets passed the Chinese drug administration's evaluation for consistency, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Friday.

The drug, which was produced by Shandong Lukang Pharmaceutical Group Saite, was tested for its quality and efficacy.

The drug is used to treat conditions caused by infection, such as bronchitis, pneumonia, sinusitis and tonsillitis, as well as sexually transmitted diseases.

The company's shares jumped 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Roche's Relapsing Multiple Sclerosis Drug Maintains Disease Activity Suppression in Mid-stage Study
data.description:
Roche's investigational drug fenebrutinib maintained a near-complete suppression of disease activity and disability progression in patients with relapsing multiple sclerosis for up to two years after treatment.

The data from the phase II FENopta open-label extension study demonstrated that patients treated with the drug showed a low relapse rate and no disability progression for up to 96 weeks, according to a Friday filing. The treatment also reduced brain lesions among patients transferred from a placebo, with a safety profile consistent with the previous data.

The Swiss pharmaceutical company added that three late-stage clinical trials, including the FENhance 1 and 2 trials in relapsing multiple sclerosis and the FENtrepid trial in primary progressive multiple sclerosis, are in progress, with data expected at the end of this year.

data.title: India's Gujarat Pipavav Port jumps as Q4 profit surges
data.description:
** Shares of Gujarat Pipavav Port up ~10% at 162.5 rupees

** Port operator's Q4 consol net profit jumped 71% as a one-off charge hurt year-ago results; revenue and expenses were flat

** Stock set to rise most in a session since March 15, 2024

** Over 13 mln shares traded, 9.6x the 30-day moving avg

** Shares traded most since March 27

** Avg rating of 10 analysts is "hold" and median PT is 145 rupees, ~10% lower than current price - data compiled by LSEG

** GPPL down ~11% YTD

($1 = 85.3620 Indian rupees)

data.title: CHANGE OF COMPANY SECRETARY
data.description:
PERTH, Australia, May 30, 2025 /CNW/ - Paladin Energy Ltd ("Paladin" or the "Company") advises that Ms Melanie Williams has been appointed as Company Secretary of Paladin effective 30 May 2025. Melanie joined Paladin in February 2025 as Chief Legal Officer. Melanie is an experienced corporate and resources lawyer and Company Secretary, with substantial international and corporate governance experience.

Mr Jeremy Ryan has resigned as Company Secretary of Paladin effective 30 May 2025. The Board of Paladin thanks Jeremy for his dedication to the Paladin Group and wishes him the best in his future endeavours.

For the purpose of ASX Listing Rule 12.6, Ms Melanie Williams will be the person responsible for communications with the ASX in relation to ASX Listing Rule matters from 30 May 2025.

This announcement has been authorised for release by the Board of Directors of Paladin Energy Ltd.

SOURCE Paladin Energy Ltd

View original content to download multimedia: http://www.newswire.ca/en/releases/archive/May2025/30/c5273.html

/CONTACT:

For further information contact: Investor Relations: Head Office: Paula Raffo, Paladin Investor Relations, T: +61 8 9423 8100, E: paula.raffo@paladinenergy.com.au; Canada: Bob Hemmerling, Paladin Investor Relations, T: +1 250-868-8140, E: Bob.Hemmerling@paladinenergy.ca; Media: Head Office: Anthony Hasluck, Paladin Corporate Affairs, T: +61 438 522 194, E: anthony.hasluck@paladinenergy.com.au; Canada: Ian Hamilton, Partner, FGS Longview, T: +1 905-399-6591, E: ian.hamilton@fgslongview.com

data.title: India's Ola Electric falls after Q1 revenue decline forecast, wider Q4 loss
data.description:
** Electric scooters maker Ola Electric Mobility down 5.5% to 50.3 rupees after Q4 results

** Ola forecast decline in current quarter revenue to 8.5 billion rupees ($99.25 million), compared to 16.44 bln rupees in Q1 FY25 amid falling sales and steep discounts

** Co's Q4 loss doubled to 8.7 billion rupees vs 4.16 billion rupees a year ago, also hit by a one-time warranty expense provision

** Kotak Institutional Equities downgrades stock to "sell" from "reduce"; cuts fair value to 30 rupees from 50 rupees

** Says, core loss was "much above" its expectations and co's future hinges on scaling up volumes and a successful motorcycle foray, which faces "executive and credibility challenges"

** Ola is targeting profitability and gross margin improvement in current fiscal year

** Shares down ~41% YTD

data.title: Nippon Steel: Japan Govt to Provide Up to Y251.4B in Support
Vopak Begins Talks with Suppliers, Offtakers for LNG Import Terminal Project in Australia
data.description:
Koninklijke Vopak commenced discussions with gas suppliers and offtakers as preparations continue for its planned liquefied natural gas import terminal project in Australia, Reuters reported Friday.

Paul Kanters, managing director of Vopak Terminals Australia, told the news platform that a final investment decision for the project is expected in 2026 or 2027, with operations projected to start in 2029, subject to regulatory approvals and commercial contracts.

Kanters added that the Dutch tank storage company is in final talks with a provider as it hopes to secure a floating-storage regasification unit designated for the project, noting that securing one has been "extremely difficult."

data.title: Samvardhana Motherson International Target Price Raised to INR170 From INR155 by Nomura >517334.BY
